learning_outcomes.description: Evaluate the complexities and strategic importance of International Human Resource Management in driving organisational success across diverse environments.
Integrate into IHRM solutions the perspectives of diverse stakeholders, including Australian Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples.
Recommend evidence-based IHRM solutions to diverse professional audiences.
Foster collaborative team dynamics to enhance self-awareness and diversity literacy for positive organisational cultures.

assessments.task_description:
Form: One individual closed-book examination comprising 30 multiple-choice questions worth 0.5 marks each and two essay questions worth 7.5 marks each. The examination is worth a total of 30 marks.

Medium: Exclusively via the Inspera platform; to be completed in-person during class time.

Timing:

Trial Run - Scheduled for August 18/19 (in Week 4). Topics covered: 3 sample questions revolving around Chapter 1 and 2 content. Duration: 15 minutes. The trial run is designed to help you become familiar with the exam interface and process before the Week 8 examination. It is not graded and will not count towards your final mark. It is strongly encouraged that all students participate in this trial, as it provides an opportunity to test your technology setup and address any issues ahead of the assessment.

In-Semester Exam - Scheduled for September 15/16 (in Week 8). Topics covered: 30 multiple-choice questions and two essay questions on Chapter 1, 2, 5, 6, 7, and 8. Duration: 90 minutes. One A4 sheet of handwritten notes, double sided, is permitted during the exam.

Late arrivals: Beginning the exam late does not make students eligible for an alternative exam. Once you log in, you must complete the exam. Make the most of the time you have left.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: Exams submitted after the end of the submission time will incur a late penalty.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
The International Assignment Consulting Report is a comprehensive assessment expecting student teams to select, analyse, and address a contemporary human resource management challenge in an emerging market context.

The report is worth 40 marks.

Team membership is determined using a random sampling method. A peer assessment follows the submission of this assessment, which may impact individuals' scores for this assessment item.

The assessment is supported by a non-graded Massive Open Online Course (MOOC) team training module.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit the report through the designated Turnitin link on Blackboard's Assessments section.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Week 11 Tue - Week 12 Wed

During your scheduled class time.
assessments.task_description:
The International Relocation Advisory Pitch requires each individual to present live on a human resource management challenge in an emerging market context. Each presentation is related to their respective Team Consulting Report. At the discretion of each individual, the Consulting Report feedback may be incorporated into the presentation. The presentation is worth 30 marks.

Please Note: The presentation will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The pitch will be delivered live in class and recorded for quality assurance purposes. No PowerPoint slides are required.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Introduction to Course

Course Introduction & Chapter 1 (Dowling et al.)

By the end of this week, you will:

  1. Understand what is meant by the term globalisation. 
  2. Be familiar with the main drivers of globalisation.
  3. Be able to better appreciate the changing nature of the global economy.
  4. Be able to define key International Human Resource Management (IHRM) terms.
  5. Understand the expatriate management evolution.
  6. Be able to outline differences between domestic and IHRM.
  7. Be alert to the increasing complexity and potential challenges in IHRM.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Introduction to Course Course Introduction & Chapter 1 (Dowling et al.) By the end of this week, you will: Understand what is meant by the term globalisation. Be familiar with the main drivers of globalisation. Be able to better appreciate the changing nature of the global economy. Be able to define key International Human Resource Management (IHRM) terms. Understand the expatriate management evolution. Be able to outline differences between domestic and IHRM. Be alert to the increasing complexity and potential challenges in IHRM.

learning_activities.topic:
Cultural Competence in Global HR

Chapter 2 (Dowling et al.)

By the end of this week, you will:

  1. Be familiar with the array of national culture definitions.
  2. Understand how culture has implications on IHRM principles.
  3. Be able to discover the need to build cultural literacy skills.
  4. Appreciate the variety of tools available to build your cultural literacy.
  5. Be empathic to cultural literacy dilemmas.
  6. Review and reflect on cultural literacy learning and experiences.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Cultural Competence in Global HR Chapter 2 (Dowling et al.) By the end of this week, you will: Be familiar with the array of national culture definitions. Understand how culture has implications on IHRM principles. Be able to discover the need to build cultural literacy skills. Appreciate the variety of tools available to build your cultural literacy. Be empathic to cultural literacy dilemmas. Review and reflect on cultural literacy learning and experiences.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Talent Identification and Selection - Part 1

Chapter 5 (Dowling et al.), Part A

By the end of this week, you will:

1. Be able to define International HRM as a combination of complex mechanisms in the process of operating globally.

2. Understand the different types of staffing policies organisations may adopt.

3. Understand the significance of expatriate and inpatriate assignment management and be able to articulate the evolution of these assignments.

Please note that Wednesday, 12 August is EKKA Day. As this is a public holiday, there will be no scheduled class on that day. Instead, the Course Coordinator will make the lecture recording from the Tuesday session available for you to access at your convenience.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Global Talent Identification and Selection - Part 1 Chapter 5 (Dowling et al.), Part A By the end of this week, you will: 1. Be able to define International HRM as a combination of complex mechanisms in the process of operating globally. 2. Understand the different types of staffing policies organisations may adopt. 3. Understand the significance of expatriate and inpatriate assignment management and be able to articulate the evolution of these assignments. Please note that Wednesday, 12 August is EKKA Day. As this is a public holiday, there will be no scheduled class on that day. Instead, the Course Coordinator will make the lecture recording from the Tuesday session available for you to access at your convenience.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Talent Identification and Selection - Part 2

Chapter 5 (Dowling et al.), Part B

By the end of this week, you will:

1. Address the factors underpinning international assignee failure.

2. Understand the factors involved in selecting an international assignee.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Global Talent Identification and Selection - Part 2 Chapter 5 (Dowling et al.), Part B By the end of this week, you will: 1. Address the factors underpinning international assignee failure. 2. Understand the factors involved in selecting an international assignee.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Performance and Career Management

Chapter 6 (Dowling et al.)

By the end of this week, you will:

1. Understand multinational performance management at the global and local level.

2. Analyse factors associated with expatriate performance, including compensation package, task and role, headquarters’ support, host environment factors, and cultural adjustment.

3. Explore performance management of expatriates and non-expatriates, and for those on non-standard tasks and assignments such as commuter and virtual.

4. Explore issues related to the performance appraisal of international employees.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Global Performance and Career Management Chapter 6 (Dowling et al.) By the end of this week, you will: 1. Understand multinational performance management at the global and local level. 2. Analyse factors associated with expatriate performance, including compensation package, task and role, headquarters’ support, host environment factors, and cultural adjustment. 3. Explore performance management of expatriates and non-expatriates, and for those on non-standard tasks and assignments such as commuter and virtual. 4. Explore issues related to the performance appraisal of international employees.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Training and Development

Chapter 7 (Dowling et al.)

By the end of this week, you will:

1. Understand the role of training and development in adjusting and retaining international assignees.

2. Address re-entry and career-related issues. 

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Global Training and Development Chapter 7 (Dowling et al.) By the end of this week, you will: 1. Understand the role of training and development in adjusting and retaining international assignees. 2. Address re-entry and career-related issues.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Compensation

Chapter 8 (Dowling et al.)

By the end of this week, you will:

  1. Examine the complexities that arise when firms move from compensation at the domestic level to compensation in an international context.
  2. Detail the key components of an international compensation program.
  3. Outline the two main approaches to international compensation and the advantages and disadvantages of each approach.
  4. Examine the special problem areas of taxation and benefit concerns.
  5. Examine the corporate and expatriate return on investment for international assignment types.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Global Compensation Chapter 8 (Dowling et al.) By the end of this week, you will: Examine the complexities that arise when firms move from compensation at the domestic level to compensation in an international context. Detail the key components of an international compensation program. Outline the two main approaches to international compensation and the advantages and disadvantages of each approach. Examine the special problem areas of taxation and benefit concerns. Examine the corporate and expatriate return on investment for international assignment types.

learning_activities.topic:
Team Consulting Project Workshop

Participate in an in-class Team Consulting Project progress consultation, where each team workshops their project by presenting updates on key milestones, findings, and challenges. Full team attendance is expected. Students will engage with the course coordinator and peers to receive feedback, clarify expectations, and align on next steps to ensure the project stays on track for final submission.
learning_activities.topic_description: Team Consulting Project Workshop Participate in an in-class Team Consulting Project progress consultation , where each team workshops their project by presenting updates on key milestones, findings, and challenges. Full team attendance is expected. Students will engage with the course coordinator and peers to receive feedback, clarify expectations, and align on next steps to ensure the project stays on track for final submission.

learning_activities.topic:
Team Presentation Workshop & Developing Your Global Career

The Presentation Workshop is an interactive, in-class session where student teams practice delivering their presentations in a supportive environment. Each team will present a draft version of their talk, receive constructive feedback from peers and the course coordinator, and reflect on areas for improvement. The focus is on refining delivery, clarifying key messages, and building confidence ahead of the final assessment.

Students will explore strategies for building an international career pathway, including how to identify global work opportunities, adapt to cross-cultural work environments, and leverage international experiences. The session also covers practical tools such as creating a globally competitive CV, networking across borders, and understanding the skills employers value in a global context.

learning_activities.topic:
Consulting Project Team Presentations

Student consulting teams will present their project findings during Weeks 11 and 12. Presentation times will be allocated during the semester. Following the presentations, students will engage in reflective analysis, comparing and contrasting both the content of the reports and the presentation approaches adopted by their peers.
